observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer’s Office reconcile the PPE accounts recorded in the books with the inventory report on actual physical count, to establish the correctness and reliability of the balances of PPE accounts as presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile account with the records of the Province of Iloilo and make necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the accounts as presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review the JEV of the fund transfers and trace up the unreconciled balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make necessary adjustments, where appropriate, to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials concerned to maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund allocated for implementation of various projects, programs and activities to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipality review the prior year’s DF and revert savings, if there are any, from completed projects pursuant to Section 322 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to ensure that the planned PPAs for disaster risk reduction and management are fully implemented on target implementation to enhance the Municipality’s preparedness to natural and man-made disasters.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the collections as revenue and the disbursements as expense for transactions pertaining to the implementation of Solid Waste Management (SWM) programs and make the necessary adjusting entry to record the unutilized balance of garbage fee collections at year-end. The Municipal Accountant could also set up a trust liability for easy monitoring of SWM collections and disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials concerned appropriate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment in the Annual Budget for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) in order to cater the needs and uphold the rights and interests of the children in the Municipality.
We likewise recommended the refund of the overpayment of remittance of employer’s contribution amounting to P 419,569.18 for CY 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Reclassify the fund transfer from Subsidy to LGUs to Due from Other LGUs; Monitor and require the liquidation reports from the recipient LGUs; Comply with the requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that the MPDO submit copy of the agreed policy and procedures in the monitoring of PAPs and the corresponding report thereon.

We recommended that the Municipality submit the necessary documentary requirements enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to support the transfer of unused balance/excess amounts of fund transfer from Trust Fund to General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) and instruct the Municipal Accountant to document the request for write-off pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the employee concerned to submit a copy of the authority/approval of the Secretary of the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) regarding the purchase of the above-mentioned motor vehicle and report to the said agency the transaction pertaining to its acquisition, if such action was not made as required under the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular 2014-155.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish the Municipal Treasurer the list of the intra-agency receivables/payables so the latter can settle them immediately to clear the books of these accounts.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant  and
Equipment (RPCPPE) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later  than
   January 31 of each year.

We also recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  and  Treasurer’s  Office
reconcile the PPE accounts recorded in the books with the  inventory  report
on actual physical count, to establish the correctness  and  reliability  of
the balances of PPE accounts as presented in the financial statements.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 20-002 dated February 24, 2020.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  assign  personnel  to  process  the
Certificate of Title of the lands under the name  of  the  Municipality  and
ensure  that  these  are  registered  under  the  Torrens  Title  System  to
ascertain ownership and protect the interest of the  Municipality  over  the
properties.

We also recommended that proper reconciliation between the  records  of  the
Accounting Office and Assessor’s Office be made  so  that  Land  account  is
recorded completely, valued  correctly,  and  presented  accurately  in  the
financial statements.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 19-015 dated July 25, 2019.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned  officers  and
employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash  advances  whose purpose
had  already  been  served  and  strictly  adhere  to   the   aforementioned
provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445  and  pertinent  provisions  of  COA
Circular  No.  97-002  and  COA  Circular  No.  2012-004  to  ensure  proper
granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We also recommended that the Accounting Department issue demand letters  and
exhaust all means  to  establish  the  accountability  of  the  lumped  cash
advances.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 19-017 dated July 26, 2019.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  provide  the  necessary  mandatory
positions of LDRRMO and comply with the guidelines and provisions set by  RA
No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-
1 dated April 4, 2014,  so  that  functions  and  responsibilities  relative
thereto will be carried out.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 19-016 dated July 26, 2019.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct the MENRO-Designate to update the list of  enrollees  for  garbage
  collection based on the categories provided in Municipal Ordinance  No. 29-
  S-2012 or the Comprehensive Solid Waste Management Ordinance.


b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to  strictly  enforce  the  collection  of
  garbage fees based on the updated list of enrollees to  be  submitted  by
  the MENRO-Designate.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-001 dated January 30, 2020.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all  officials  concerned  to
maximize  the  utilization  of  the  20%  Development  Fund  allocated   for
implementation of various projects, programs and activities to help  achieve
the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes  of  the
Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipality review the  prior  year’s  DF  and
revert savings, if there  are  any,  from  completed  projects  pursuant  to
Section 322 of               R.A. No.  7160.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-003 dated March 25, 2020.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant
to check the validity of the non-moving prior years’ balances of Due to  BIR
and Due  to  GSIS  accounts  and  reconcile  the  amounts  withheld/deducted
against the amounts remitted, and direct the Municipal  Treasurer  to  remit
immediately the valid payables.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-004 dated May 19, 2020.

Recommendation

We recommended the refund of P100,000.00 in accordance with  the  rules  and
regulations in the settlement of accounts and to revert to the General  Fund
the transfer of P100,000.00.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-005 dated June 23, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the specific PPE accounts identified and recognize depreciation for those PPE acquired during the year. Prior years’ depreciation expenses should be recorded and should be charged against the Prior Period Adjustment account, since they were not recorded in the years they were incurred. Subsequently, the Prior Period Adjustment should be closed to Government Equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the cashbooks and the accounting records regularly so that Cash in Local Treasury is accurately presented in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments on the amounts pertaining to cash shortages included in the account Cash Local Treasury, should be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements and should be recorded to the appropriate account as soon as the specific details concerning these have been identified.

We recommended the refund of P100,000.00 to the Province of Iloilo and to utilize future funds granted for the specific purpose for which it was intended for. Moreover, it is recommended that the other P100,000.00 taken from the trust fund be replaced/refunded to the said fund.
observations.reason: P100,000.00 was refunded to Iloilo Province but the Municipality used government funds instead of payment from those responsible of the disallowance. Additional finding is issued in this year’s audit report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor inform the DOH of the non-implementation of the project for the intended beneficiary and take the appropriate action to rectify the situation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials concerned comply with the implementation of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Systems and other prescribed procedures and guidelines under R.A. No. 9003 and strictly implement the policies provided in its Comprehensive Solid Waste Management Ordinance.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor and MENRO- Designate provide the necessary support in enjoining all barangays to establish their own MRF and to strictly implement waste segregation at source.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit fully and promptly the inter-agency payables to the concerned government agencies in compliance with the above-mentioned laws and regulations.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the validity of the non-moving prior years’ balances of Due to BIR and Due to GSIS accounts and reconcile the amounts withheld/deducted against the amounts remitted and remit immediately the valid payables.

We recommended that the Local School Board follow the proper planning and budgeting procedures for the SEF to ensure strategic and efficient utilization of fund intended for the priority programs, projects and activities of various schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all officials concerned see to it that future operations of the Municipal Public Utilities and Economic Enterprise will generate enough revenues to cover its expenses and to pay its advances from general fund.
We also recommended that rules, regulations and guidelines in LGU transfers/advances to LEEs/PUs provided in LBC No. 100 be strictly observed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board stop the practice of charging expenditures to the Special Education Fund of projects and activities not provided in DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund and record the transfer to the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books at the end of the year.
We also recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the guidelines set by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and MDRRMO prioritize the projects and activities enumerated in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, dated March 25, 2013 for disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.
We also recommended that other maintenance and operating expenses for general services and administrative purposes should be charged to the General Fund of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials concerned establish the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS). Moreover, LGU GFPS shall assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code or ordinance.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the M&E Team. Further, the Municipality adhere to the guidelines on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women provided by the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 and any updates concerning GAD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances whose purpose has already been served and strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
We also recommended that the Accounting Department exhaust all means to establish the accountability of the lumped cash advances.
We further recommended the immediate refund of all unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, withhold the salaries of all officers and employees with outstanding cash advances until the amounts involved are fully restituted.

We recommended that appropriate organization and composition of LDRRMO must be complied in accordance with the guidelines and provisions set by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report in compliance with Section 12.2 of the revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184.
